query: Poland Orka submarine program winner decision 2026 South Korea Hanwha, Poland K2 tank second phase contract 2026 value timing
answer:
Research complete. Key findings:

**Orka Submarine — Winner is Saab (Sweden), NOT Hanwha.** The research premise was incorrect. Poland selected Sweden's Saab A26 (preferred design Nov 2025; contract signed June 29, 2026) for ~$4.8B / SEK 47B for 3 A26 Blekinge-class submarines, deliveries through 2038. **Hanwha Ocean lost** despite bidding ~$5.6B and South Korea offering a free Jang Bogo-class submarine transfer. Fincantieri (Italy) also lost.

**K2 Tank — Hyundai Rotem confirmed.** The "second executive contract" was signed **August 1, 2025** (~$6.5B, 180 tanks + 81 support vehicles). The relevant **2026** milestone is the **April 28, 2026** Bumar-Łabędy/Hyundai Rotem local-production agreement defining work/payment division — 61 K2PL tanks and 72 support vehicles to be assembled in Poland, first K2PL rolling off the Gliwice line in 2028. All under the 2022 framework for up to 1,000 K2-family tanks.

The full report and structured data (13 data points, 12 sources) have been delivered.

sources.title: Poland signs $4.8B deal for three Saab A26 submarines
sources.snippet: Saab signed a $4.83 billion contract with Poland's State Treasury Armaments Agency on June 29, 2026, for three A26 submarines. Final deliveries scheduled for 2038; Poland to borrow HMS Sodermanland as gap-filler.

sources.title: Poland Signs SEK 47 Billion Contract for Three Saab A26 Submarines, Ending the Decade-Long Orka Tender
sources.snippet: On 29 June 2026 Poland's Armaments Agency signed a contract with Saab for three A26 submarines under the Orka programme, ~SEK 47 billion (~$4.8 billion), deliveries to 2038. Fallback July talks with Fincantieri and Hanwha Ocean not triggered.

sources.title: Poland selects Sweden's Saab A26 as future submarine
sources.snippet: The Polish government officially chose Saab's A26 Blekinge-class submarines for the Orka program, expected 10 billion zloty ($2.52 billion) investment for three submarines.

sources.title: Hanwha Ocean bids for Poland's Orka submarine program
sources.snippet: Hanwha Ocean making a strong push to win Poland's Orka submarine project worth approximately 8 trillion won ($5.6 billion).

sources.title: South Korea to transfer submarine to Poland for free
sources.snippet: South Korea plans to provide its first-generation Jang Bogo-class submarine to Poland at no cost to support a domestic bid for Warsaw's next-generation submarine program.

sources.title: It's crunch time for Poland to pick a new submarine design
sources.snippet: Defense officials sent RFIs to six countries; PM Tusk's end-of-year deadline for picking a winner. Delegations visited France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Sweden, and met South Korean officials.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs $6.5 billion agreement with Poland for second batch of K2 main battle tanks
sources.snippet: Signed August 1 at Bumar-Labedy in Gliwice. 116 K2GF tanks delivered 2026-2027; 64 K2PL tanks 2028-2030, final assembly of 61 K2PL at Bumar-Labedy.

sources.title: Poland orders more K2 MBTs, support vehicles
sources.snippet: The Polish Armaments Agency signed the second executive contract with Hyundai Rotem for 180 more K2 MBTs at Bumar-Labedy in Gliwice on 1 August.

sources.title: Poland signs USD6.5bn tank deal with Hyundai Rotem
sources.snippet: Poland ordered an additional 180 K2 tanks from Hyundai Rotem, plus 81 support vehicles and a complete logistical package.

sources.title: Poland signs agreement to produce South Korean K2 tanks domestically
sources.snippet: April 28, 2026: Bumar-Labedy and Hyundai Rotem signed agreement defining division of work/payments; Bumar-Labedy to assemble 61 K2PL tanks and 72 support vehicles, first K2PL in 2028.

sources.title: Poland to begin production of first K2PL tanks as part of new agreement with South Korea
sources.snippet: 2022 framework agreement establishes procurement of 1,000 K2-family tanks: 180 base-model K2s and 820 K2PL units licensed for Polish production through 2034.

sources.title: Poland Bought Up to 1,000 K2 Tanks From South Korea. The Real Prize Was the Factory
sources.snippet: In April 2026 the domestic production plan moved from paper to factory floor; Warsaw using its largest-ever arms purchase to rebuild a domestic tank industry.

key_facts: Poland selected Sweden's Saab A26 (NOT Hanwha Ocean) for the Orka submarine program; preferred design chosen November 2025.
Orka contract signed June 29, 2026 in Gdynia: 3 Saab A26 Blekinge-class submarines worth ~SEK 47 billion (~$4.8 billion), deliveries through 2038.
Losing Orka bidders included Hanwha Ocean (South Korea, KSS-III, ~$5.6B bid) and Fincantieri (Italy); Germany's tkMS, France's Naval Group, Spain's Navantia were also considered earlier.
South Korea offered a free first-generation Jang Bogo-class submarine transfer (Nov 2025) to support Hanwha's bid, but Poland still chose Saab.
Poland leased Sweden's HMS Södermanland (~SEK 1.9 billion) as a gap-filler submarine; crew training began August 2026, transfer ~2027.
K2 tank SECOND executive contract signed August 1, 2025 with Hyundai Rotem: 180 tanks + 81 support vehicles, worth ~$6.5 billion.
K2 second contract split: ~116-117 K2GF tanks from South Korea (2026-2027) and ~63-64 K2PL tanks assembled in Poland (2028-2030).

April 28, 2026: Bumar-Łabędy and Hyundai Rotem signed a local-production cooperation agreement defining work/payment division — 61 K2PL tanks and 72 support vehicles to be assembled in Poland; first K2PL in 2028.

K2 program sits under a July 27, 2022 framework for up to 1,000 K2-family tanks (180 base K2 + ~820 K2PL through ~2034); the first 180-tank executive contract (2022, all South Korea-built) is fully delivered.
Poland is South Korea's largest arms export customer; the K2 deal's strategic aim is rebuilding a domestic tank industry via technology transfer at Bumar-Łabędy.
